entity.summary: Solana-based decentralized perpetuals and trading protocol that remained in a critical limited state after the April 2026 exploit. Drift's June 2026 recovery update said the team was rebuilding the protocol for a security-first relaunch and that the exchange had not yet been brought fully back online.
entity.notes: Retain limited rather than active or dead. The official domain and recovery pages are live, but the June 3 recovery update describes relaunch as future work and says the team is taking steps to bring the platform back online. Existing exploit and suspension events are not duplicated.

expected.summary: Solana-based decentralized perpetuals and trading protocol that entered a critical limited state in April 2026 after a major exploit and suspension of deposits and withdrawals.
expected.notes: Do not mark dead yet. The April 2026 exploit and deposit / withdrawal suspension are material enough for HEI because they changed platform availability, but no final shutdown marker is confirmed.

changes.summary: Solana-based decentralized perpetuals and trading protocol that remained in a critical limited state after the April 2026 exploit. Drift's June 2026 recovery update said the team was rebuilding the protocol for a security-first relaunch and that the exchange had not yet been brought fully back online.
changes.notes: Retain limited rather than active or dead. The official domain and recovery pages are live, but the June 3 recovery update describes relaunch as future work and says the team is taking steps to bring the platform back online. Existing exploit and suspension events are not duplicated.

evidence.title: Drift Recovery Update – June 3, 2026
evidence.notes: First-party update says Drift's focus remained relaunching the exchange and described work to bring the platform back online, supporting limited rather than active or dead classification.

evidence.title: Drift official protocol site
evidence.notes: Current official domain remains reachable and links to Drift's application, documentation, and incident-recovery updates.
